runs.text: Control room operators must record critical information quickly while keeping their language clear and calm. The caller may be distressed, so every typed note should capture the location, the immediate danger, the people involved, and any changes that happen while help is being arranged.

A report arrives about smoke coming from the third floor of an office block near the station. The caller says several people are waiting outside, one person may still be inside, and access is through the rear service road beside the loading bay.

When information changes during an incident, update the log without losing the original details. Mark uncertain facts clearly, confirm spellings where possible, and keep the timeline accurate so responders can make decisions from a reliable record.

The caller is reporting a road traffic collision at the junction near Eastbridge Market. Two vehicles are blocking the northbound lane, one driver is trapped, and fuel appears to be leaking onto the road. The caller can hear a child crying in the second vehicle but cannot safely approach because traffic is still moving around the scene. Police, fire, and ambulance support may be required. Record the location, hazards, number of vehicles, possible injuries, and any access problems for responding units.
